mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate for equity cash flows, boosting stock valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This trend signal is always active.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p < 0.05) when FEDFUNDS is falling compared to rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a 5.0% threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns is 50% or higher, or average returns are not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer and residential demand, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The signal is active when both proxies are above their respective 3-month moving averages.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST above their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: When both proxy conditions are satisfied, the 60-day forward cumulative return is negative more than 40% of the time, or the Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 2% signals an imminent recessionary contraction in supply capacity, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. This effect is regime-specific to industrial contraction periods.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls more than 2% in one month
failure_condition: Following a month-over-month INDPRO drop >2%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not negative, or the hit rate (negative returns) is below 55% with a sample size of at least 10 events.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate below 3.5% signals an aggressive easing cycle that provides immediate liquidity support to financial markets, boosting equity prices over the next 5 trading days. This threshold-triggered signal captures very short-term monetary accommodation effects.
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days after FEDFUNDS crosses below 3.5%, the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the median return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real gross domestic product growth signals a healthy expanding economy that drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon. This trend signal is always active as a baseline proxy for economic momentum.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.1 over any 10-year rolling window, or the signal has zero predictive power (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls increase month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously decreases, the labor market is in a confirmed expansionary phase, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > prior month AND UNRATE < prior month
failure_condition: When both conditions are satisfied, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive less than 55% of the time, or the average return is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05) compared to the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the federal funds rate and increase in retail sales signals that monetary easing is effectively stimulating consumption, a powerful combination that drives equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days. This interaction captures the joint monetary-demand channel.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreasing month-over-month AND RSAFS increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio over any 3-year window.
